# IFC Test Fixtures

This folder holds the IFC files used by the round-trip test harness
(`tools/tests/round_trip.py`) and (future) CI integration.

## Files

| File | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `spatial_codes_ok.ifc` | Tiny valid IFC4 with Pset_StingSpatialCodes + Pset_StingTags fully populated, passes every IDS spec. | *generated by `tools/tests/round_trip.py --generate-fixture` — not committed* |
| `spatial_codes_mismatch.ifc` | Variant of the above with deliberate tag-to-storey mismatch (`Pset_StingTags.Level = "L99"` but containing storey LevelCode = "L01"). Used to verify STING-side equality closeout fires. | *to be generated* |
| `tag_grammar_invalid.ifc` | Tag values that fail pattern restrictions: `Discipline = "XYZ"`, `Sequence = "1"` (not zero-padded). Used to verify `sting-tag-grammar.ids` fails as expected. | *to be generated* |

## Generation

Fixtures are produced by `tools/tests/round_trip.py --generate-fixture`,
which uses `ifcopenshell.api` to mint minimal IFC4 files. The script
documents the call sequence but the full implementation lands once
the `stingtools-core` Python package is wired up — for now this is a
scaffold.

Fixtures are committed to git only when small (< 50 kB) and useful for
manual inspection. Otherwise the generator script is the source of
truth.

## Adding a new fixture

1. Add an entry to the table above.
2. Extend `tools/tests/round_trip.py` with a new `--generate-fixture FIXTURE_NAME`
   variant.
3. Commit the generator change; the fixture itself stays uncommitted
   unless it's a useful manually-edited base.
